behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names all relevant files (BabelPlugin.ts, Imports.ts, Options.ts, Program.ts, ValidateNoUntransformedReferences.ts, Environment.ts), the specific dead code elements (ValidateNoUntransformedReferences, CompileProgramMetadata, retryErrors, 'client-no-memo', compileProgram return value), and the classes/functions involved (ProgramContext, compileProgram, BabelFn). All test assertions reference items discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what is broken (dead code, unused abstractions, obsolete options) but does not prescribe exact code changes like 'delete line X' or 'change return type to void'. A developer must reason about what specifically to remove.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The tests check for behavioral/structural properties (file deleted, type alias absent, property removed, return type is void, no import references) rather than gold-specific implementation details. Any correct dead code removal approach would pass these tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests use a mix of approaches: TypeScript AST parsing via Node.js (test_compileprogram_returns_void, test_retryerrors_removed_from_class, test_schema_rejects_client_no_memo), file existence checks (test_validate_file_deleted), text searches for imports (test_validate_not_imported), and actual build/typecheck/test execution (test_repo_typecheck, test_repo_build, test_repo_jest_unit). The AST-based tests and subprocess execution tests go well beyond simple text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests check for the absence of specific code elements that exist in the original codebase (ValidateNoUntransformedReferences.ts file exists, CompileProgramMetadata type is declared, retryErrors property exists, 'client-no-memo' is in the enum, compileProgram returns non-void). A stub or no-op would not pass these tests since the original code must be actively modified.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors described in instruction.md are tested: unused ValidateNoUntransformedReferences (test_validate_file_deleted, test_validate_not_imported), compileProgram return value (test_compileprogram_returns_void, test_compile_result_not_captured), retryErrors field (test_retryerrors_removed_from_class), BabelFn import (test_babel_fn_import_removed), 'client-no-memo' option (test_schema_rejects_client_no_memo, test_client_no_memo_removed_environment).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section headers (fail-to-pass behavioral, fail-to-pass structural, pass-to-pass static, pass-to-pass repo CI), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining each test's purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves dead code removal, not structured data production.

no_typos.reason: All filenames, paths, function names, and variable names in instruction.md and tests match correctly (ValidateNoUntransformedReferences, CompileProgramMetadata, ProgramContext, retryErrors, client-no-memo, compileProgram, BabelFn, etc.).

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly names the exact files to modify ('Files to Look At' section lists all 6 files), specific type names (CompileProgramMetadata), class fields (retryErrors), function names (compileProgram), and the exact obsolete option ('client-no-memo'). This goes beyond describing what's broken and significantly narrows the search space, especially listing ValidateNoUntransformedReferences.ts as a file to look at when part of the task is discovering it should be deleted.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not an agent config edit task.
